steps.title: Fastest path: graph or solve the equation
steps.content:
Type the equation into Desmos as written:
$$38{x}^{2}=38 \left(9\right)$$Graph or solve it directly and read the x-value that works. The requested value is $-3$.

steps.title: Divide both sides by 38
steps.content:
The coefficient 38 appears on both sides. Divide to simplify:
$$x^2 = 9$$

steps.title: Take square roots to find both solutions
steps.content:
Taking the square root of both sides:
$$x = \pm\sqrt{9} = \pm 3$$
The two solutions are $x = 3$ and $x = -3$. The negative solution is $x = -3$.
